output: Overfitting happens when a model learns training-specific noise instead of general patterns, causing weak performance on unseen data.

LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) fine-tunes large models by injecting small trainable low-rank matrices into selected layers, dramatically reducing trainable parameters and GPU memory needs while retaining strong task adaptation performance.
8-bit quantization reduces memory footprint and can speed training, making large models practical on smaller GPUs with minimal quality trade-offs for many tasks.
